<!-- Generated by a 6-persona Claude council + 5-angle web research workflow (2026-06).
     44 agents, 27 proposals, 26 verified feasible against the real code, 1 killed.
     Bias: make Design Book easier for an AI agent to drive and diagnose. -->

# Design Book — Council Roadmap

The 26 verified proposals cluster around one root cause: **the default compose path produces imageless, invisible-media slop, and the one place that could catch it — the coherence scorer and the save-gate — is blind to it.** Everything below sharpens the wedge: a local, free, deterministic facts-lab that lets a pixel-blind agent verify and *gate* design quality with zero tokens.

---

## Top of the list: the #1 thing

### Teach the coherence scorer the authenticity law, so slop can no longer score 100

**Problem.** `checkCoherence` (`frontendmaxxing/mcp-server/server.js:981-1016`) scores only 4 string patterns (hardcoded-hex, unblessed-duration, px-radius, slop-hover). It never measures imagery, gradients, or motion — so an imageless gradient wireframe scores **100** and sails through the SAVE-GATE. A `genre:'ecommerce'` compose returns 0 `<img>`, 0 `<script>`, 4 grey `.s-reveal` boxes, coherence 100. That is the exact AI tell the product condemns, and the single chokepoint the model can't talk past is currently waving it through.

**Why #1.** Every front-door fix, imagery nudge, and tool-description edit is downstream of this. The scorer is the *one deterministic surface* all three consumers delegate to (`/api/compose`, `/api/coherence`, `lib/vault.js:110`), and the gate already rejects on `coh.score<70` — so folding authenticity *into* the score auto-arms the gate with **no new gate wiring**. Fix this and the agent gets a scored, fix-tagged authenticity readout on every compose, instantly.

**The concrete change.**
1. `frontendmaxxing/mcp-server/server.js` `checkCoherence` — on the original (un-scrubbed) HTML, cheap-regex `imageCount` (`<img`/`<picture`/`<canvas`/drawn inline `<svg>`/`background-image:` ≠ none), `gradientCount` (linear/radial/conic), `motionSignals` (advisory). Fold in:
   - **HARD:** `gradientCount>=2 && imageCount===0` → `-40` penalty, tell `GRADIENT-HEAVY-NO-IMAGERY`, fix "call book_generate_image for the hero and drop a gradient." This is the only thing that should breach the gate; -40 guarantees <70 from a clean 100 base.
   - **SOFT:** `imageCount===0` → `-12`; `motionSignals===0` → `-6` (advisory — fresh drafts legitimately have no script).
   - Return a new `authenticity:{imageCount,gradientCount,motionSignals,bespoke,tells[]}` block.
2. `designbook/server.js` `composeWithScore` (94,98) — widen the returned coherence to include `authenticity` (currently destructures only `{score,counts}` and would silently drop it).
3. `designbook/designbook-mcp/server.js` `coherenceLine` (63-69) — render the tells so the agent sees the deficit the instant it drafts.

**Impact 5 · Effort 2.** Composed drafts will now score in the 70s-80s (imageCount 0 → -12) — intended; it signals the deficit without auto-failing, while the gradient-heavy combo fails hard.

---

## Quick wins (low effort, high impact)

| # | Problem (one line) | Concrete change | I/E |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | The "compose N, pick best" primitive is unreachable — `/api/variants` exists but no MCP tool exposes it. | Add **`book_variants`** to `designbook-mcp/server.js` after `book_compose` (~L250): POST `/api/variants` with `seeds=[0,1,2]`, rank by coherence, return a ranked table + **only the winner's HTML** (per-seed byte counts for losers). One thin `registerTool` block; endpoint already done. | 4/1 |
| 2 | An agent orienting via `book_overview` never discovers `book_generate_image`/`book_save_asset`/`book_view` — it ships imageless gradient pages and never finds the differentiator. | Description-only edits in `designbook-mcp/server.js`: insert an imagery step into `WORKFLOW` (L118-125), add an "anti-slop tools" line to `book_overview` text (L151-161), fix the header tool list (L16-19) to name all 14. **Frame imagery as a quality norm, NOT "the gate rejects them"** — the gate does not check imagery today; lying would break trust. | 4/1 |
| 3 | The invisible-reveal blank-content bug: `.s-reveal` boxes are `opacity:0` forever — `renderComposedHtml` emits zero `<script>`, so `.is-in` is never added in *any* browser, and the reduced-motion block never resets opacity. | Two fixes in `frontendmaxxing`: (a) `structure.css` reduced-motion block (~206-233) add `.s-reveal{opacity:1!important;transform:none!important}`; (b) `renderComposedHtml` (~695) inject a ~12-line reduced-motion-guarded IntersectionObserver before `</body>`. Verify diagnose console stays clean (it will). | 5/2 |
| 4 | Save-gate rejections dump bare names: undefined-vars and overflowers carry no fix text; coherence hint is decoupled from the score; and `book_coherence` says "below 80" while the gate rejects <70 — two "good" numbers for one metric. | `designbook-mcp/server.js`: rewrite `gateFindings` (420-429) to return `{check,cause,fix}` objects (use the `overflowers[].right/width` the gate currently discards to name the widest one); render each line as `<check>: <cause> → <fix>`; keep `structuredContent.violations` as string codes (don't break the `:`-split fingerprint at 454). Align the threshold text on 70 (hard gate) / 80 (stretch). | 4/2 |

---

## High-impact builds (more effort, big payoff)

### Make the gate enforce the authenticity law (a craft-floor)
**Problem.** Even with the scorer taught (#1), the gate should explicitly name the imagery remedy so the agent generates *before* its first save instead of learning via rejection.
**Change.** `designbook-mcp/server.js` `gateFindings`: pass `html` in scope (already at call site 443-451), add `IMAGERY_MANDATORY = Set(['ecommerce','portfolio','restaurant','agency','landing'])` (`frontendmaxxing` ~L500), reject imageless visual-first genres and `.s-reveal`-with-no-script with exact remedies. Pre-warn in `book_compose`'s description so the agent generates imagery before saving. **Threshold ≥1 image, only on the mandatory set** — don't over-gate saas/blog/startup.
**Impact 5 · Effort 2.**

### book_autofill_imagery — one-call wireframe → deliverable
**Problem.** Even with the scorer flagging "0 images," the agent must hand-place each generated image. The gap between a composed wireframe and a real deliverable is manual.
**Change.** New `POST /api/autofill-imagery {slug}` in `designbook/server.js`: read saved HTML + manifest, classify empty `.s-reveal` slots (hero/product/gallery) by surrounding shell, derive per-slot prompts from `art-direction.skill.md` recipes keyed on aesthetic, call `generateImage` with bounded concurrency (each 30-60s, surface progress via SSE), swap `.s-reveal` div → `<img loading=lazy width height alt>`. Expose as MCP `book_autofill_imagery`. **Gate it optional** so an mflux outage never blocks a bug fix.
**Impact 5 · Effort 3.**

### Render-truth detection in the diagnose probe
**Problem.** A pixel-blind agent's facts say a page is fine while a human sees invisible media — `DIAGNOSE_PROBE` never reads computed `opacity`/`visibility` on content. The gate can't catch the invisible-page class.
**Change.** In `designbook/lib/inspect.js` `DIAGNOSE_PROBE`'s existing per-element sweep (~310-363, **no extra Chrome pass**): add a `render` group — `invisibleContent` (area>0 elements with text/media whose self-or-ancestor `opacity~0`/`visibility:hidden`, flagging `.s-reveal:not(.is-in)`), `collapsedSections` (<8px height). Weight it into the score; add one line to `gateFindings` rejecting `invisible-content`. Net safety net for hand-edited HTML once the compose-side reveal fix ships.
**Impact 4 · Effort 2.**

### Self-healing book_compose (deterministic auto-fix at source)
**Problem.** The first draft is blank and the agent only learns post-inspect.
**Change.** New pure `designbook/lib/autofix.js` `autofix(html)→{html,fixed[]}`, called in `composeWithScore` (`server.js:85-99`) **after** compose, **before** coherence. Repair the known-safe classes: inject reveal observer, add `data-todo-alt`/`alt=""` to real `<img>` (skip decorative mediaBox divs). Surface `autofixed[]` in `book_compose`. Inline verify (`verify:true`) is **opt-in only** — `inspect()` spawns headless Chrome (30s), and default-on would regress the "free, instant, zero tokens" compose contract.
**Impact 4 · Effort 2.**

### Make the save-gate platform-aware (mobile lens)
**Problem.** The mobile gap, not the framing the proposal first assumed: composed flows do **not** trip `hOverflow` (`.app-flow` is `overflow-x:auto`), but mobile output gets **zero HIG enforcement** — a flow with sub-44pt taps, content under the status bar, or actions above thumb-reach saves clean.
**Change.** `designbook-mcp/server.js` `book_save_page`: detect mobile via inline regex (`/class="[^"]*\bapp-flow\b|\bscr-frame\b/` — **do NOT import lib/handoff into the pure MCP client**) or `manifest.platform`. Branch the gate: mobile path runs `inspect mode:'mobile'` and a new `gateFindingsMobile` rejecting on `mob.score<70`, `smallTaps`, `safeAreaIssues`, `reachIssues`, nav gaps — **never on board-width overflow**. The mobile-lens summary fields already exist.
**Impact 4 · Effort 2.**

### Real seed diversity (so "compose N, pick best" produces distinct looks)
**Problem.** Seeds rotate only *which snippet fills a slot* (HTML comments + manifest); the visual theme is byte-identical across seeds — `book_variants` would show a flat table.
**Change.** `frontendmaxxing/mcp-server/server.js`: add `GENRE_PALETTE_SHORTLIST` (and optional font shortlist) near `GENRE_AESTHETIC` (L500) using real palette names, index 0 = today's default (seed 0 byte-identical — regression guard). In `composePage` (725-734) and `composeApp` (956-965), perturb **only unpinned axes** by `shortlist[seed % len]`. **Keep aesthetic stable** — rotating it cascades `AESTHETIC_DEFAULTS` and breaks comparability. Pinned opts/presets always win.
**Impact 4 · Effort 2-3.**

### Teach the mobile lab the anti-slop tells
**Problem.** `MOBILE_PROBE` is pure geometry — it can't see an empty `.scr-media`, a computed gradient, or `:hover` rules (touch has no hover); a three-empty-box media flow scores high.
**Change.** `designbook/lib/inspect.js` `MOBILE_PROBE` per-screen loop (~640): add provable-only detectors — `emptyMedia` (`.scr-media`/`.scr-avatar` with no image child + no bg image), `gradientCount` vs budget of 1, `hoverRules` (same-origin stylesheet scan), plus a shell-provable large-title check on feed roots. Small honest penalties into `warnings[]`/`pen`. **Scope down** the mini-player/genre check — the probe has no genre today. These only bite once consumed by the mobile gate (above).
**Impact 4 · Effort 2.**

---

## Strategic bets (ambitious, differentiating)

### A `taste` inspect mode — make "does it look good" a fact, not a screenshot
**Problem.** The agent has no deterministic fact for "is this bespoke or templated?" and must burn a `book_view`. The 5 inspect modes derive zero composition facts.
**Change.** New `TASTE_PROBE` (6th mode) in `designbook/lib/inspect.js`, rendered once at desktop, citing offenders via the existing `sel()`+`data-db-ref` convention: type-scale ratios & heading:body contrast, spacing-value rhythm, gradient-vs-real-imagery balance, and a `tells[]` array of the **4-6 robust, low-false-positive** DOM-shape fingerprints (inter/system-only stack, gradients-without-imagery, 3+ identical sibling cards, colored-left-border feature cards, glassmorphism). Wire `MODES` (691), the dispatch map (717), the mode enum (`designbook-mcp/server.js:275`), and **add a `renderTasteReport`** (or it falls through to the layout renderer and prints nothing). A `tasteScore` can later feed the gate as an optional signal. **Ship the robust tells first; treat "~16 tells" as aspirational.**
**Impact 4 · Effort 3.**

### Compose real native flows (sheets, FAB, mini-player, grouped lists, empty states)
**Problem.** Composed mobile flows are 9 flat shells of grey `.scr-media` boxes — contradicting the skill, which demands real native patterns. The `.scr-fab`/`.scr-sheet` primitives already exist in `app-shell.css` but no shell emits them.
**Change.** `frontendmaxxing/mcp-server/server.js`: (a) add `sheet`/`listFab`/`mediaPlayer`/`settingsGrouped`/`empty` archetypes to `MOBILE_SHELLS` and point `MOBILE_FLOWS` at them; (b) add `connection: tab|stack|sheet` metadata threaded into the manifest + `data-connection` attribute for the FLOW.md handoff. **Critical:** preserve the literal `<div class="scr-frame">` string or update `lib/vault.js:100/103` in lockstep — it stamps `data-db-ref` via `indexOf`. **Defer** inlining real `mobile/` components (slice c) — each ships ~31 selectors of CSS `renderComposedApp` doesn't link.
**Impact 4 · Effort 4.**

### Diversity for mobile flows (variety_seed varies the .scr HTML, not just a comment)
**Problem.** `composeApp` seed only picks a component *string* baked into a manifest comment; the rendered `.scr` HTML is byte-identical across seeds — "compose N variants and pick" is inert for mobile.
**Change.** `frontendmaxxing/mcp-server/server.js`: seed-driven aesthetic ring per genre (highest ratio, do first — palette/font/motion cascade for free), flow variants pointing at **new** `MOBILE_SHELLS` renderers (the real effort — depends on the native-flows bet above), and optional-screen presence varying flow length. Surface `variant`+`seed` in the return and manifest.
**Impact 4 · Effort 3.**

### Single-source the tool surface + invariant tests
**Problem.** Drift seams: the header lists 11 of 14 tools; `book_inspect`'s mode enum is a hand-copy of `MODES`; `book_view`'s viewport enum duplicates `VIEWPORTS`; no test covers the MCP bridge.
**Change.** Import `MODES`/`VIEWPORTS` (already exported) into `designbook-mcp/server.js`, replace literals with `z.enum([...MODES])`. Export a `TOOLS` manifest, add a parity test to `test.js`. Add invariant tests: web compose provenance (unique `data-db-ref` per section), `gateFindings` unit fixtures, all-genres smoke. **Do NOT add a startup `/api/meta` fetch** — it would make the bridge fail to launch when the core is down, regressing the friendly per-call error. **Do NOT unify the gate's 70 with the vault's 80** — they're deliberately distinct layers (hard reject vs soft advisory); just extract `GATE_COHERENCE_MIN=70`.
**Impact 3 · Effort 2.**

### Bounded-Chrome queue + render cache
**Problem.** All Chrome spawns are cold (~2s); concurrent briefs/inspects can fork unbounded; draft→inspect→save double-renders the same HTML.
**Change.** `designbook/lib/inspect.js`: a module-level FIFO semaphore (`CHROME_CONCURRENCY=2`) around the three `execFileP(chrome,…)` sites, plus a 60s render cache keyed on `hashOf(doc)+mode+sorted(names)` — **only on the `!screenshot` path** (screenshots have a PNG side-effect). The gate's diagnose render reuses the agent's prior inspect on the common path (2 renders → 1). **Skip the warm-Chrome/CDP idea** — it violates the zero-dep invariant and adds a real failure surface for marginal extra gain.
**Impact 3 · Effort 2.**

### Auto-route book_inspect to mobile mode + honest FLOW.md handoff
**Problem.** Omitting `mode` on a saved mobile page runs the web probe (2600px layout → spurious overflow). And `buildFlowHandoff` is static, iOS-only, discards the component path it already has, and hardcodes geometry.
**Change.** `designbook/server.js` `/api/inspect`: when `!body.mode` and (`manifest.platform==='mobile'` or `isMobileHtml(html)`), set `mode='mobile'`, flag `autoRoutedMobile`. In `lib/handoff.js`: widen `readScreens` to capture the component path, replace the iOS-only conventions block with an iOS|Material divergence table (design canon from the skill), make geometry honest, derive a cheap inferred flow from the per-screen `tab` field. **The real screen graph is gated on unbuilt connection metadata** — ship the rest now.
**Impact 3 · Effort 2 (each).**

---

## If we only do three things

- **Teach the coherence scorer the authenticity law** (`checkCoherence` + `composeWithScore` + `coherenceLine`). This single change makes the deterministic gate reject the imageless-gradient slop it currently scores 100 — arming the one surface the model can't talk past, with no new gate wiring. *(I5/E2)*

- **Fix the invisible-reveal bug + add the imagery craft-floor** (`structure.css` reduced-motion reset + reveal observer in `renderComposedHtml` + `IMAGERY_MANDATORY` in `gateFindings`). This stops the default draft from silently shipping blank media and forces real imagery on visual-first genres — closing the exact gap a competitor demo would visibly win. *(I5/E2)*

- **Surface the anti-slop toolset + expose `book_variants`** (`book_overview`/`WORKFLOW`/header description edits, frame imagery as a norm not a false gate rule; new thin `book_variants` over the orphaned `/api/variants`). This makes the imagery tools and parallel-exploration primitive *discoverable* from the front door — without these, the agent never finds the differentiator no matter how good the gate is. *(I4/E1 each)*

These three are mutually reinforcing: the scorer detects the deficit, the gate+reveal fix forces the remedy, and the front-door edits make the remedy discoverable. Combined effort is small (all Effort 1-2), all verified feasible with no architecture rewrite.
